questions.question: 1 (a) The metre ruler is clamped in position near to the spring. Do not change the position of the metre ruler. (i) Measure the unstretched length l 0 of the spring. Record l 0 in cm in Table 1.1. Do not include the loops at the ends of the spring in your measurement. The value l 0 is the length of the spring when the load L i
questions.text: 1 (a) The metre ruler is clamped in position near to the spring. Do not change the position of the metre ruler. (i) Measure the unstretched length l 0 of the spring. Record l 0 in cm in Table 1.1. Do not include the loops at the ends of the spring in your measurement. The value l 0 is the length of the spring when the load L i

parts.answer: l values increasing and  l e values correct all l and e values to nearest mm  axes correctly labelled and right way round  appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid between plotted points)  plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots  well- j udged line and thin line
triangle method clearly shown on graph using at least half of distance between extreme plots 0625/51 May/June 2024

questions.question: 2 (a) Measure the height h O of the illuminated object. parts.answer: I to at least 2 decimal places and  A V to at least 1 decimal place and  V units V and A seen correctly used in (a) and (b) correct calculation of r unit  / cm
new set of results present with r value within 10% of r
statement to match results j ustification to match statement and results.
at least three additional values all values quoted are more than 0 and up to 100 cm
correct symbol for variable resisto r 0625/51 May/June 2024 – 1.6 (or 14 – 16) matching unit (cm or mm)

value given for h I with unit h I = h O to within 10% / bright object OR move screen slowly / back and forth / approach clearest position from both directions (and average) 0625/51 May/June 2024 MP1 additional apparatus: (metre) rule r / tape (measure) MP2 method mark 1: add a load and measure the amount of bending MP3 method mark 2: repeat with a different load applied OR different length (overhanging) OR a different number of layers of wood in composite strip MP4 variable kept constant: load (if varying length mentioned in MP3 ) OR length (if varying load mentioned in MP3 ) MP5 variable kept constant: number of layers of wood MP6 table: with clear columns for deflection with matching unit and for chosen variable – all with quantity and unit (or no unit for number of strips) MP7 conclusion: plot graph of deflection against chosen variable OR graph / bar chart of deflection against number of layers OR an answer which suggests comparing values in table of chosen variable and deflection
